findings.title: Venue adapters call a generic executeLeg hook instead of typed Base venue ABIs
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the generic IRouterLegExecutor path with per-family adapters that decode constrained payloads and call the actual Solidly, Uniswap V2, Slipstream, Uniswap V3, and V4/Universal Router ABIs; keep mocks behind tests only.

findings.title: Final minimum is checked before settlement, so transfer-tax or malicious tokenOut can under-deliver
findings.suggested_fix: Either restrict launch fixtures to non-deflationary final tokens or verify recipient and fee-recipient balance deltas after settlement and revert when the delivered net amount is below minAmountOutAfterFee.

findings.title: Deployment script can broadcast before the non-launchable fixture gate is cleared
findings.suggested_fix: Gate deployment on Base chain id, FameRouterFixtureManifest.isLaunchable(), and the required live validation checks, or split this into an explicitly local-only draft deployment script.

residual_risks: Frozen Base pool and route fixtures are still placeholders, so real venue metadata, hook constraints, and route executability remain unproven.
Owner-enabled targets remain a high-trust boundary until the allowlist is populated exclusively with audited production router addresses.
Native ETH output/input behavior has only mock coverage; real WETH/ETH venue paths still need fork validation.
testing_gaps: forge test --match-path 'test/router/*' passed unit and fixture-placeholder tests but the Base fork test failed because network access was unavailable for BASE_RPC.
No tests exercise real Solidly, Uniswap V2, Slipstream, Uniswap V3, or V4/Universal Router calldata against pinned Base forks.
No malicious or fee-on-transfer ERC20 tests cover final output settlement, approval cleanup edge cases, or balanceOf anomalies.
No deployment-script test or dry-run enforces the launch gate before broadcasting.
